batch_summary: Deed [122] PDF pulled (LC Vol. 13, Pg. 301, Inst# DR-00013-00301). CRITICAL INDEX ERROR CONFIRMED: LC index shows grantor as 'BOUNDS T W' but the deed text unambiguously reads 'I J. W. Bounds.' The grantor is J. W. Bounds, not T. W. Bounds. This is a warranty deed with vendor's lien, executed September 26, 1890 (not November 1891 — the 1891 date was the filing date), conveying 158 7/10 acres of the John Boyd Survey from J. W. Bounds to James Brown & Sam Adams for $2,600 in six promissory notes. The metes-and-bounds confirm this is the same parcel as deeds [106] and [108] (beginning 500 vrs W of SE corner of Boyd League). This finding strongly implies the [108] grantee was also J. W. Bounds (not T. W. Bounds) — chain logic requires it, as J.W. could not sell the parcel without having received it. The Theophilus Walton Bounds hypothesis from the index data is now substantially weakened. ISSUE-32 significantly advanced; ISSUE-40 item [122] marked PULLED.

02_PROPERTY_INVENTORY.md.timestamp_summary: Deed [122] PDF — Boyd Survey Critical Finding #4 updated ([108] parcel disposition now confirmed via [122])

operations.description: Update Critical Finding #4 — the [108] parcel's disposition is now confirmed by [122] PDF (J.W. Bounds sold it to Brown & Adams in 1890)
operations.find: 4. **JW Bounds' fate of the [108] 156-acre parcel is unknown.** JW Bounds received the entire ~156-acre Boyd Survey parcel as a gift in 1889. No deed from JW Bounds or his heirs reconveying this land to any family member has been found. The [113] 80-acre Boyd portion is confirmed as a separate part of the survey (different metes-and-bounds, Barry family source). The [108] parcel's disposition remains open — see ISSUE-32.
operations.replace_with: 4. **The [108] 156-acre parcel was sold out of the family chain by J.W. Bounds in 1890.** Deed [122] (PDF confirmed 2026-03-31) documents J.W. Bounds selling the entire ~158.7-acre Boyd Survey parcel (same metes-and-bounds as [106]/[108]) to James Brown & Sam Adams on Sept 26, 1890, for $2,600 via six vendor's lien notes. ⚠️ LC index for [122] shows grantor as "T.W. Bounds" — PDF proves this is an index error; actual grantor is "J. W. Bounds." This confirms the [106]/[108] parcel permanently left the family chain ~18 months after the [108] gift. No reversion. See ISSUE-32.

03_DEED_CHAIN_SUMMARY.md.timestamp_summary: Deed [122] PDF pulled — J.W. Bounds (NOT T.W.) → James Brown & Sam Adams, 158.7 ac John Boyd Survey (executed 1890-09-26); INDEX GRANTOR ERROR confirmed; ISSUE-32 advanced

operations.description: Replace [122] index-only stub with full PDF-confirmed entry
operations.find:
#### Deed [122] — T. W. Bounds → Brown James et al. (1891-11-13, Limestone)
- **Transaction type**: Fee simple conveyance — John Boyd Survey, 158 acres
- **Citation**: LC Vol. 13, Pg. 301, Inst# DR-00013-00301
- **Property**: 158 acres, John Boyd Survey
- **Significance**: Reconveyance of the [106]/[108] 158-acre Boyd parcel — T.W. Bounds selling approximately 2.5 years after receiving it as a gift. The [106]/[108] parcel permanently left the family chain in November 1891. This definitively rules out the [106]/[108] parcel as the upstream source of deed [24] ~42.8 acres. Also confirms consistent use of "T W" across multiple index entries, corroborating ISSUE-32 T.W. Bounds name finding.
- **Disposition status**: **DISPOSED — NO REVERSION.** Pull PDF to confirm grantor name (T.W. Bounds — must match [108] grantee to complete the chain) and verify no reversion clause.
- **Action needed**: Pull LC Vol. 13 Pg. 301. Resolve ISSUE-32 name question simultaneously.
operations.replace_with:
#### Deed [122] — J. W. Bounds → James Brown & Sam Adams (1890-09-26, Limestone) — **PDF CONFIRMED** ⚠️ **INDEX GRANTOR ERROR — NOT T. W. BOUNDS**
- **Transaction type**: Warranty deed with vendor's lien — surface + minerals, John Boyd Survey, 158 7/10 acres (or less)
- **Citation**: LC Vol. 13, Pg. 301, Inst# DR-00013-00301 [PDF CONFIRMED: deed [122], 2026-03-31]
- **Property**: 158 7/10 acres (or less), part of the John Boyd Survey, Limestone County. Beginning at a point 500 varas west of the SE corner of John Boyd League, stake in south line; N with J. V. Bounds West line to Tehuacana Creek about 1900 vrs; up creek with meanderings to NE corner of 150-acre tract sold Lou Foster (known & belonging to Atkinson & Foster); S 166¾ vrs to stake in south line of Boyd League and SE corner of said tract ⚠️ [deed says "50 acre tract" here — scrivener's error for "150"]; E with Boyd's south line 500 vrs to POB. Plus two acres given: one to [?] Banks at SW corner, one to State of Texas at SE corner.
- **Interest**: Surface + minerals — full warranty; vendor's lien retained until notes paid ("when this deed shall become absolute")
- **Consideration**: $2,600 total via six promissory notes: (1) $400 due Jan 1, 1891; (2) $440 + 10% interest due Dec 9, 1891; (3) $440 + 10% interest due Dec 9, 1892; (4) $440 + 10% interest due Dec 9, 1893; (5) $440 + 10% interest due Dec 9, 1894; (6) $440 + 10% interest due Dec 9, 1895
- **Parties**: Grantor: J. W. Bounds (Limestone County). Grantees: James Brown & Sam Adams (Limestone County). ⚠️ **INDEX GRANTOR ERROR**: LC index (Inst# DR-00013-00301) identifies grantor as "BOUNDS T W" — PDF confirms grantor is unambiguously **J. W. Bounds** (deed text: "I J. W. Bounds"). The index misread cursive "J" as "T."
- **Acknowledgment**: September 26, 1890 (same date as execution), before J. E. Longbotham, Notary Public, Freestone County. Same notary as deeds [108], [109], [110].
- **Recording**: Filed November 13, 1891 at 4:30 PM; recorded November 14, 1891 at 9:15 AM; W. P. Brown, Co. Clk. / H. E. Ellis, Deputy, Limestone County. ⚠️ **Clerk name**: "W. P. Brown" — prior Limestone County instruments ([105], [109]) show "W. F. Brown" as County Clerk (1887–1890). Either a different Brown succeeded as clerk, or one middle initial is a transcription error.
- **Metes-and-bounds confirmation**: Description matches deeds [106] and [108] — beginning 500 vrs W of SE corner, N to Tehuacana Creek, W along creek, S, E 500 vrs to POB. Same ~158-acre parcel. The [106] description references "J.H. Sparks" for the 1-acre SW carve-out; [122] references "[?] Banks" — these may be successive owners of the same corner parcel.
- **⚠️ 150 ac / 50 ac discrepancy**: The Atkinson & Foster tract is called "a 150 acre tract" in the first reference and "the above referred to 50 acre tract" in the second. "50" is almost certainly a scrivener's error for "150."
- **J. V. Bounds boundary reference**: East boundary follows "J. V. Bounds West line" — confirms J.V. Bounds held Boyd land immediately east of this parcel as of September 1890. Consistent with ISSUE-48 (J.V. Bounds Boyd source unlocated).
- **Significance**: (a) **INDEX GRANTOR ERROR**: LC index shows "T.W. Bounds" but deed says "J. W. Bounds." The prior ISSUE-32 analysis citing "consistent use of 'T W' across multiple index entries" is now undermined — at least one "T W" entry ([122]) is demonstrably wrong. Chain logic requires [108] grantee = [122] grantor = J. W. Bounds; the [108] index entry ("BOUNDS T W") is almost certainly the same misread. (b) **Execution date September 26, 1890** — approximately 18 months after the [108] gift (March 27, 1889), not "approximately 2.5 years" as previously calculated from filing dates. (c) Confirms the [106]/[108] parcel permanently left the family chain — full warranty deed, no reversionary interest. (d) **Same notary** (J. E. Longbotham, Freestone County) as [108] and [109] — all three Bounds instruments from 1889–1890 used the same Freestone County notary.
- **Disposition status**: **DISPOSED — NO REVERSION.** Full warranty deed with vendor's lien retained until notes paid; no reversionary interest to Bounds family.
- **Action completed**: PDF pulled; ISSUE-40 item [122] marked PULLED. ISSUE-32 significantly advanced (index grantor error confirmed — J.W. not T.W.).
- **Margin note**: "J. W. Bounds / [?:Sold] / (as Brown / & Sam Adams / Nov 13 91 / \" 14\" \" "

operations.description: Add deed [122] PDF analysis update to ISSUE-32
operations.anchor: - **Status**: OPEN — upgraded to HIGH PRIORITY given the T.W./J.W. name discrepancy and the new 1877 gifts requiring J.E. and J.V. Bounds chain research.
operations.content:
- **Deed [122] PDF analysis** (2026-03-31):
    - ⚠️ **INDEX GRANTOR ERROR CONFIRMED**: Deed [122] (LC Vol. 13, Pg. 301) PDF pulled. The deed text reads "I J. W. Bounds" — the LC index entry "BOUNDS T W" is **demonstrably wrong**. The index misread cursive "J" as "T" (or vice versa).
    - **Chain logic resolution**: Deed [108] gifted the [106] 156-net-acre Boyd parcel to "J. W. Bounds" (per deed [108] transcription) or "T. W. Bounds" (per LC index). Deed [122] shows J. W. Bounds selling that identical parcel (same metes-and-bounds: beginning 500 vrs W of SE corner of Boyd League). J.W. Bounds could not have sold the parcel in [122] unless he received it in [108]. **Therefore [108] grantee = J. W. Bounds.** The LC index entry for [108] ("BOUNDS T W") is almost certainly the same clerk misread that produced the [122] index error.
    - **Impact on T.W./Theophilus hypothesis**: The index-based identification of the [108] grantee as Theophilus Walton Bounds is now substantially weakened. Both index entries ([108] and [122]) that showed "T W" are compromised — at minimum [122] is proven wrong. The deed [108] transcription's reading of "J. W. Bounds" is corroborated by [122] and should be treated as the presumptive correct reading pending pull of the [108] original.
    - **Remaining action**: Pull [108] original at LC Vol. S, Pg. 582 to definitively confirm grantee initial. This is no longer HIGH priority for chain purposes — the chain is logically complete (J.W. received via [108], sold via [122]) — but should be confirmed for record.
    - **Status**: OPEN — downgraded from HIGH to MEDIUM PRIORITY. The T.W./J.W. name question is substantially resolved in favor of J.W. by chain logic and [122] PDF evidence. Remaining research: (a) confirm [108] original; (b) J.E. and J.V. Bounds Boyd chain research (ISSUE-41, ISSUE-48) remains independent.
